fields.button_left: Call for global pause on recursive self-improvement
fields.button_right: Publish graph showing 52× speedup already happening in your office

narrative_summary: Anthropic is simultaneously pressing both buttons — publicly demanding a global verifiable pause on recursive self-improvement while their own internal Claude Mythos model has already achieved a 52× speedup on AI training code inside Anthropic HQ.
rationale: Candidate 3 earns its specificity score by naming Claude Mythos, citing the exact 52× speedup, and grounding the joke in 'your office' — the right button's phrasing 'publish graph showing 52× speedup already happening in your office' is the sharpest closing beat of the five, turning the hypocrisy from a restatement into a concrete, self-incriminating image. The other candidates either soften the specificity or let the right button collapse into a generic ship-it line.
story_title: When AI Builds Itself: Our progress toward recursive self-improvement
place: inside a broken vending machine that only accepts crypto
hat_pair: crash helmet with a tiny windshield wiper on the visor
foam dartboard hat with a plastic dart stuck in the bullseye
